title: Hell's Gate Campsites
description: Public KWS campsites near the gorge — budget tents, stars, and morning bike rides before day-trippers arrive.
highlights:
KWS public camp
Bike mornings
Gorge access
Matatu reachable
map_query: Hell's Gate National Park camping Kenya
directions: Same as Hell's Gate day access; request campsite at Elsa Gate.
road_conditions: Murram near gate; fine for camping vehicles.
safety_and_conditions: Wildlife in camp — store food securely. Baboons active.
what_to_carry: Tent, sleeping bag, cooking kit, warm layer.
travel_tips: Rent tent near Naivasha town if needed — KES 800–1,500/night.
facilities: Basic KWS bandas, pit toilets, fire rings

title: Lake Elementaita Community Camp
description: Lakeside community campsite with flamingo views — quieter and cheaper than Naivasha waterfront lodges.
highlights:
Flamingo views
Community camp
Stargazing
Budget lakeside
directions: Matatu to Naivasha or Gilgil, boda/taxi to Elementaita shoreline camps.
safety_and_conditions: Hippo and buffalo near shore at night — camp in designated areas.
travel_tips: Ask camp host for latest flamingo viewing spots.

title: Kedong Ranch Camp
description: Open grassland camping popular with cyclists and overlanders between Naivasha and Mai Mahiu.
highlights:
Open plains
Cyclist friendly
Group camping
Low fees
directions: Matatu towards Mai Mahiu, drop at Kedong Ranch sign; short walk in.
safety_and_conditions: Open exposed site — secure tent in wind. No shade midday.
what_to_carry: Tent pegs, sun shade, plenty of water.
travel_tips: Popular weekends — arrive Friday to secure spot.

title: Oloiden Campsite
description: Quiet crescent lake off Lake Naivasha — budget camping and hippo spotting without park fees.
highlights:
Hippo lagoon
Birding
Budget camp
Kayak option
directions: Matatu to Naivasha, boda along south lake road to Oloiden camps.
safety_and_conditions: Hippos at night — do not walk shore in dark.

title: Camp Ndunda Falls
description: Riverside camp near Ndunda Falls in Embu — forest shade and waterfall swim on a budget eastern route.
highlights:
Waterfall swim
Riverside tents
Forest shade
Embu gateway
directions: Matatu Nairobi–Embu, taxi to Ndunda Falls camp.
safety_and_conditions: Slippery rocks at falls; supervise swimming.
travel_tips: Weekends busy with Nairobi campers — book ahead.

title: Lake Bogoria Public Campsite
description: Geothermal lakeshore camping with flamingos and hot springs — budget alternative to lodge stays.
highlights:
Hot springs
Flamingos
KWS camp
Stargazing
map_query: Lake Bogoria National Reserve camping Kenya
directions: Bus to Nakuru or Kabarnet route, matatu to Loboi gate.
safety_and_conditions: Hot springs burn — test water. Baboons near camp.
what_to_carry: Tent, warm layer, swim sandals for springs.
travel_tips: Morning flamingo viewing best before wind picks up.

title: Mount Kenya Bandas — Sirimon
description: Basic mountain bandas at Sirimon gate — start Lenana trek without expensive lodge rates.
highlights:
Trek start point
Mountain bandas
Budget overnight
Guide hub
map_query: Sirimon Gate Mount Kenya bandas Kenya
directions: Bus to Nanyuki, taxi to Sirimon gate bandas.
what_to_carry: Sleeping bag, warm clothes, trek food.
travel_tips: Book bandas via KWS when registering trek.

title: Oloolua Nature Trail Camp
description: Forest campsite in Nairobi's southern green belt — cheapest overnight nature fix inside the city counties.
highlights:
Forest camp
Nature trail
Near Nairobi
Youth-friendly
directions: Matatu/boda to Karen, enter Oloolua forest gate.
travel_tips: Book camp spot at gate on arrival.

title: Kijabe Forest Camp
description: Escarpment forest camp on the Rift edge — cool air and pine trails an hour from Nairobi.
highlights:
Escarpment views
Cool climate
Forest trails
Budget camp
directions: Matatu Nairobi–Naivasha route, drop Kijabe, walk to forest camp.
safety_and_conditions: Cold nights — pack warm sleeping bag.
travel_tips: Combine with Rift viewpoint day hike.

title: Lake Chala Camp
description: Crater lake camp with turquoise water — budget tents and swim after the descent trail.
highlights:
Crater swim
Border views
Budget tents
Adventure camp
directions: Bus to Taveta, camp operator pickup or boda to lake.
safety_and_conditions: Steep descent with gear — porter optional.
travel_tips: Camp fee often includes guide for descent.
